reviewer_attack: TED is a complex pipeline, not a new method.
response_strategy: TED's central contribution is the claim-aware event object and evidence-gated claim ceiling, not a single score.
release_response: Emphasize object-level contribution and ablation evidence.
residual_limitation: Complexity remains; workflow diagram must be clear.

reviewer_attack: How is TED different from tradeSeq, CellRank, GSVA, AUCell, VISION, CINEMA-OT or WOT?
response_strategy: Those methods solve upstream tasks; TED consumes or compares their outputs and formalizes the downstream event-and-claim layer.
release_response: TED does not replace trajectory inference, fate mapping, module scoring or OT matching.
residual_limitation: Direct wrappers cover representative packages but not every possible external workflow.

response_strategy: Use task-specific baselines plus direct package wrappers for tradeSeq/GSVA/AUCell/POT and a package-complete Docker baseline environment.
release_response: Both baselines and TED detect signal, but TED improves event-type accuracy, artifact rejection and overclaim control.
residual_limitation: Direct external package execution is complete in Dockerfile.baselines; package versions and exit codes are recorded in the execution manifest.

response_strategy: Adversarial sweeps test low signal, dropout, imbalance, missing time, batch confounding and artifacts.
release_response: Low-identifiability settings are downgraded rather than overclaimed.
residual_limitation: More external ground-truth datasets would help.

reviewer_attack: No wet-lab validation means no causal mechanism.
response_strategy: Agree; TED encodes this as a claim ceiling.
release_response: GSE271399 is Level 3.5, not Level 4; GSE292039 does not pass Level 4B.
residual_limitation: Strict Level 4 requires future rescue experiments.

reviewer_attack: TED is only tuned to GSE271399/ZSCAPE.
response_strategy: Generalization panel spans GSE199308, GSE123013, GSE127202 and GSE157977.
release_response: TED preserves evidence levels across full, lite and adapter analyses.
residual_limitation: Some systems remain adapters/scaffolds, not full TED.

response_strategy: Axis-free modules, marker-dependency audits and curated-vs-axis-free concordance test marker dependence.
release_response: Curated and data-driven modules are compared; markers do not automatically become claims.
residual_limitation: Axis-free modules require interpretation and annotation.

response_strategy: Use stratified streaming, sampling-confidence shrinkage and prefix-vs-stratified concordance.
release_response: Claim is embryo-block/pseudobulk event grammar only, not cell-type fate loss.
residual_limitation: Full matrix refit with cell-state annotation would be stronger.

reviewer_attack: GSE123013 weakens the plant generalization claim.
response_strategy: It is intentionally presented as claim discipline: signal is detected but downgraded under stress controls.
release_response: TED refuses overclaiming when stress/housekeeping/wound controls dominate.
residual_limitation: Not a strong plant root mechanism.

response_strategy: It is explicitly an adapter demonstration at guide-barcode level.
release_response: No target-gene-specific neural fate claim is made.
residual_limitation: Needs guide-target map and cell-state annotation for stronger claims.

reviewer_attack: GSE292039 should upgrade the rescue claim.
response_strategy: Gene mapping completed, but rescue-style alignment did not pass.
release_response: Public functional alignment was tested and did not support Level 4B.

response_strategy: Compress to dynamic event grammar and avoid one-to-one orthology claims.
release_response: The claim is process-level grammar, not conserved causal genes.
